# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14-slim AS builder

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package*.json to use it as npm scripts does
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies with npm
RUN npm install

# Copy all other files/folders to build directory
COPY . .

# Build and copy the configuration file
COPY config/config.json config/config.json
COPY src/**/*.config.* .

# Expose port 3000, where our application listens
EXPOSE 3000

# Run application with node command. This will also start the server and expose it.
CMD [ "node", "src/index.js" ]

# Build stage complete, move to production image

FROM node:14-slim AS prod
RUN mkdir -p /app/node_modules && \
    cp -r ./node_modules /app/node_modules/  # Copy over the installed modules to a fixed directory

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /app/package*.json ./
COPY --from=builder /app/.env .
COPY --from=builder /app/src/index.js src/index.js
COPY --from=builder /app/config/* config/

RUN npm install
# Use the user node: non-root user to prevent potential security risks when running your application
USER node

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

# Build docker image for deployment on private registry (dockerhub)
ARG REGISTRY="your-private-registry.com"
BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 && \
        docker build --rm -t ${REGISTRY}/myapp:latest .
